title: Slow Steamed Russian Mushroom Dumplings
description: Earthy and tender, these dumplings are a nostalgic Russian dish featuring dried red chillies and steamed to perfection, making them a great option for a Buddhist temple friendly meal.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, rehydrated mushrooms, crushed dried red chillies, chopped onion, and minced garlic.
Gradually add water to the mixture and knead until a dough forms.
Divide the dough into small balls and flatten each ball into a disk shape.
Place the dumplings in a steamer basket, leaving some space between each dumpling.
Steam the dumplings over boiling water for 20 minutes.
While the dumplings are steaming, heat the vegetable oil in a small pan over medium heat.
Add the salt and black pepper to the oil and stir to combine.
Once the dumplings are cooked, remove them from the steamer and brush with the seasoned oil.
Serve the dumplings hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
